role: A central motion-design token module that defines shared animation constants and resolves transitions for the interface.
capabilities: Exports spring presets (gentle, snappy) and a material eased-out cubic
Exports MotionFeel type and a transition resolver that maps role + feel to a Transition
Honours prefers-reduced-motion by returning a near-zero duration transition
substance_why: The file implements concrete token values, a typed resolver function, reduced-motion handling, and a reusable variant set.
interactions_present: Reduced-motion check via window.matchMedia at transition resolution time
Spring/eased feel selection passed into transition()
interactions_missing: No live reduced-motion listener or reactive update when the preference changes
No actual animated components or orchestration/sequencing logic
No per-role colour transition variant beyond duration
No validation or fallback for invalid role values
No integration with the mentioned S4.3 taste toggle (only accepts feel as an argument)
performance_risks: Each transition() call re-runs matchMedia; negligible but avoidable if preferences were cached or observed
No hazards visible because the code restricts animation to transform+opacity as per its own mandate
reuse_why: The file is a well-scoped, single-source-of-truth motion token layer whose constants and resolver should survive the redesign with minor API cleanup.
decomposition_hints: Keep curves/durations as pure token exports
Keep transition resolver as the single semantic entry point
Consider extracting variant primitives (fadeRise) into a separate variants file if more disclosure patterns are added
